'''Callum McLelland''' (born 16 September 1999) is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in the [[RFL League 1]].

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and has spent time on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
McLelland was born in [[Pontefract]], West Yorkshire, England. He qualified to represent Scotland through his grandfather, born in [[East Ayrshire]].<ref name="Leeds signing">{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2018-08-14 |title=Leeds confirm signing of rising star Callum McLelland |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-confirm-signing-of-rising-star-callum-mclelland |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

McLelland attended [[Castleford Academy]]. He played his junior rugby league for his local team [[Castleford Lock Lane]] before being picked up by the [[Castleford Tigers]] academy.

After progressing through the Tigers academy system, in November 2017 McLelland left the club to cross codes and join [[Edinburgh Rugby|Edinburgh Rugby Union]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2017-11-28 |title=Edinburgh sign Callum McLelland from Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.talkingrugbyunion.co.uk/edinburgh-sign-callum-mclelland-from-castleford-tigers/19336.htm |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Talking Rugby Union}}</ref> This was through the [[Scottish Rugby Union]]'s 'Scottish Qualified' scheme. McLelland said, "Lads in rugby league don’t get opportunities like this, to travel the world and represent your country constantly through the year. It’s a life-changer for my family to be able to live up in Edinburgh and I can really start to make a name for myself."<ref name="Leave for union">{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=2017-12-08 |title=England's Academy captain issues warning as he leaves for union |url=https://www.totalrl.com/englands-academy-captain-issues-warning-leaves-union/ |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Total Rugby League}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
In August 2018, after less than a year with [[Edinburgh Rugby|Edinburgh Rugby Union]], McLelland switched codes again and signed for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] on a three-and-a-half-year contract for an undisclosed fee.<ref name="Leeds signing" /><ref name="Leeds beat Cas">{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=2018-08-14 |title=Leeds beat Cas to snap up McLelland |url=https://www.totalrl.com/leeds-beat-cas-to-snap-up-mclelland/ |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Total Rugby League}}</ref><ref name="Meet Callum McLelland">{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Gareth |date=2019-02-21 |title=Meet Callum McLelland, the code-hopping teen ready to make an impact at Leeds |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/meet-callum-mclelland-code-hopping-14030537 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=The Mirror}}</ref>

On 12 April 2019, McLelland made his professional debut for Leeds against [[Workington Town]] in the [[Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-04-12 |title=Challenge Cup: Leeds Rhinos 78-6 Workington Town |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47866259 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 11 May, he scored his first try for Leeds against the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the following round of the cup.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Townsend |first=Joe |date=2019-05-11 |title=Challenge Cup: Bradford Bulls 24-22 Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48189282 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He made his [[Super League]] debut on 16 May against the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-05-16 |title=Super League: Castleford add to Leeds frustrations with crushing five-try 30-8 win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48267674 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

McLelland made a further 13 appearances for Leeds, scoring 1 try, with his playtime severely restricted by injury.<ref name="Leave Castleford">{{Cite web |date=2023-06-21 |title=McLelland to leave Castleford |url=https://www.totalrl.com/mclelland-to-leave-castleford/ |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Total Rugby League}}</ref>

==== Featherstone Rovers (dual registration) ====
In June 2019, McLelland joined [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Featherstone Rovers]] through their dual registration arrangement with Leeds.<ref name="Featherstone">{{Cite web |last=White |first=Alex |last2=Shaw |first2=Matthew |date=2019-10-04 |title=McLelland loving life at Featherstone as Grand Final beckons |url=https://www.totalrl.com/mclelland-loving-life-at-featherstone-as-grand-final-beckons/ |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Total Rugby League}}</ref> He made 12 appearances and scored 4 tries, appearing in every play-off game as Rovers eventually finished runners up in the [[Million Pound Game]].<ref name="Featherstone" />

McLelland continued to play for Featherstone at the beginning of the 2020 season, making a further 3 appearances and scoring 1 try.<ref name="RLRKC" />

===Castleford Tigers===
On 11 October 2021, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced that McLelland would re-join his hometown club on a two-year contract, with an option of a further two years.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-10-11 |title=McLelland comes back to Cas |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7666 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-10-11 |title=Callum McLelland: Castleford re-sign half-back |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58875981 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=2021-10-12 |title=Callum McLelland explains reasons for Castleford return |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/callum-mclelland-explains-reasons-for-castleford-return/ |accessdate=2021-10-13 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> McLelland was assigned the squad number 16 for the 2022 season,<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-01-21 |title=Tigers reveal 2022 squad numbers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7841 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> although a patella tendon injury sustained while at Leeds persisted and prevented him from making an appearance.<ref name="Leave Castleford" />

In March 2023, McLelland spoke of his desire to repay the club for their support during his injury rehabilitation.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2023-03-02 |title=Callum McLelland wants to repay Castleford faith after injury nightmare |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/callum-mclelland-castleford-injury-nightmare |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> However, in June, head physiotherapist [[Matt Crowther]] revealed that McLelland had unfortunately "suffered a further setback in his recovery".<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-06-13 |title=Tigers player and injury update |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8635 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> On 21 June, Castleford confirmed that the two parties had come to an agreement on the early termination of his contract.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-06-21 |title=McLelland departs the Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8647 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref name="Leave Castleford" /> McLelland would depart the club with immediate effect, having not made a senior appearance, to pursue a new venture outside of the sport.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2023-06-21 |title=Castleford Tigers playmaker to 'start new venture outside of the sport' following injury nightmare |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-playmaker-to-start-new-venture-outside-of-the-sport-following-injury-nightmare |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=2023-06-21 |title='I'm gutted': Callum McLelland calls time on Castleford Tigers career after injury hell |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/im-gutted-callum-mclelland-calls-time-on-castleford-tigers-career-after-injury-hell-4192124 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

===Midlands Hurricanes===
On 8 December 2023, it was announced that McLelland had signed for [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total Rugby League|date=8 Dec 2023|title=Ex-Castleford Tigers and Leeds Rhinos halfback Callum McLelland joins new club|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-and-leeds-rhinos-halfback-callum-mclelland-joins-new-club/}}</ref>

== Representative career ==

=== England rugby league ===
McLelland was named in [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s elite under-15s squad in August 2015, captaining and scoring in the 52-12 victory against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] under-16s at [[Taffs Well RFC|Taffs Well]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-08-19 |title=England Youth ready to test themselves against Wales |url=http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/32936/england-youth-ready-to-test |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150905111904/http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/32936/england-youth-ready-to-test |archive-date=2015-09-05 |website=The RFL}}</ref>

McLelland captained the [[England national rugby league team|England]] Academy side in 2017.<ref name="Leave for union" /><ref name="Leeds beat Cas" /> He started at stand-off against [[France national rugby league team|France]] under-18s at [[Stade de la Méditerranée]] on 9 October, where he kicked the late winning drop goal, and at [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]] on 13 October, in a 1-1 series draw.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2017-10-13 |title=England Academy fall to late defeat in France |url=http://www.rugby-league.com/article/51227/england-academy-fall-to-late-defeat-in-france |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171015100354/http://www.rugby-league.com/article/51227/england-academy-fall-to-late-defeat-in-france |archive-date=2017-10-15 |website=The RFL}}</ref>

=== Scotland rugby union ===
McLelland's move to [[Edinburgh Rugby|Edinburgh Rugby Union]] through the 'Scottish Qualified' programme saw him play for the [[Scotland national under-20 rugby union team#2018|Scotland national under-20 rugby union team]]. He made his debut in the [[Six Nations Under 20s Championship|Under-20s Six Nations]] and also went on to play in the [[World Rugby Under 20 Championship|World Rugby Under-20s Championship]],<ref name="Meet Callum McLelland" /> making a total of 8 appearances.<ref name="Leeds signing" />

=== Return to England rugby league ===
In December 2018, following his return to rugby league, McLelland re-established his place in the [[England national rugby league team|England]] Academy side. He started at stand-off against the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] at [[Leigh Sports Village]] on 8 December and at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley Stadium]] on 14 December, helping to secure a 2-0 series win for England Academy.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2018-12-15 |title=England Academy 18 Australia Schoolboys 6: England youngsters provide proof future is in safe hands at Headingley |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/england-academy-18-australia-schoolboys-6-england-youngsters-provide-proof-future-is-in-safe-hands-at-headingley-187040 |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref name="Meet Callum McLelland" />

=== Scotland rugby league ===
In October 2019, McLelland was named in [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]'s 22-man squad for the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup qualification#Second round|European qualifiers]] of the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-10-18 |title=Scotland name 22 man squad for World Cup qualifiers |url=https://www.scotlandrl.com/2019/10/18/scotland-name-22-man-squad-for-world-cup-qualifiers/ |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Scotland Rugby League}}</ref> On 26 October, he made his senior international debut and scored a try in Scotland's 86-0 victory over [[Serbia national rugby league team|Serbia]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-10-26 |title=Scotland move closer to World Cup qualification with 86-0 win over Serbia |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/scotland-move-closer-to-world-cup-qualification-with-86-0-win-over-serbia |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> On 1 November, he scored 2 tries in Scotland's 42-24 victory over [[Greece national rugby league team|Greece]], securing qualification for the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-11-02 |title=World Cup Qualifier – Greece 24 v Scotland 42 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/world-cup-qualifier-greece-24-v-scotland-42/ |access-date=2023-06-26 |website=Total Rugby League}}</ref>
